webSearchFindings.semiconductorBearMarket: The PHLX Semiconductor Index has entered a technical bear market (>20% decline from its June peak), newly pressured by Chinese startup Moonshot AI's release of the Kimi K3 open-weight model (2.8T parameters, reportedly near-comparable performance to Anthropic's Fable 5 at much lower cost) — a fresh structural concern about Western AI-infrastructure valuations, not just short-term noise. NVDA is testing the psychologically important $200 support level as a result.

AAPL.status: Pulled back to $326.59 on 2026-07-20 (-2.14%) as flagged as a risk in the 2026-07-18 decision (overbought RSI, KeyBanc downgrade) — still well within the stop/target band, held unchanged.

decisionProcess: Applied the system prompt v2's 7-role deliberation using the qualitative web-search findings above. The semiconductor sector has moved from 'selloff' to a confirmed technical bear market (>20% off June peak), freshly pressured by a low-cost Chinese AI model (Moonshot's Kimi K3) that raises structural questions about Western AI-infra valuations rather than just short-term profit-taking. Separately, TSLA and GOOGL both report Q2 earnings tomorrow (2026-07-22) after market close — a textbook binary event risk a stop-loss can't protect against, ruling out any new position in either direction. Every new-position candidate was vetoed at the Risk Manager step. The existing AAPL long pulled back exactly as the prior decision's overbought/analyst-split flag anticipated, but remains comfortably within its stop/target band and continues unchanged.
